title: Three horizontal bars represent the three structural layers of every Precept rule: the declaration (state/event), the guard condition, and the governing transition. The declaration is blue; the guard is amber; the transition is bright green and heaviest — because the `->` is what the definition ultimately certifies. One contract in three strokes.
conceptualBridge: The three weighted bars mean Precept's definition has exactly three governing layers because every `from S on E when G -> T` rule has a declaration, a condition, and a transition — the bars are those layers, arranged in execution order.
decision: Rejected: despite passing the average threshold (4.0), the distinct silhouette score of 3 makes it fail — three horizontal bars is the generic 'hamburger menu' shape; without color it reads as nothing specific to Precept. The concept of three structural layers is valid but this form cannot carry it.
